typography.description: All typography is driven by settings_schema.json. Four font roles with per-heading controls for size, line-height, letter-spacing, and case.

accent.usage: Display text, typically H1 only. Can be a decorative/serif font distinct from heading.

headingPresets.description: Each heading level (h1-h6) plus paragraph has independent settings for size, line-height, letter-spacing, case, and which font role to use.
headingPresets.fontRolePerHeading: Each heading can pick its font role independently. H1 typically uses 'accent', H2-H4 use 'heading', H5-H6 use 'subheading'.

lineHeightPresets.description: Mapped to actual CSS values in theme-styles-variables.liquid, not direct pixel/percentage values

cssVariables.application: Applied via .text-block.h{n} > * selectors using CSS variables

figmaMapping.description: How to map Horizon typography to Figma text styles and variables
figmaMapping.textStyles: Create one text style per heading level (H1-H6) + paragraph. H1 uses accent font, H2-H4 use heading font, H5-H6 use subheading font, paragraph uses body font.
figmaMapping.variables: Create font-size variables for each level. Do NOT create line-height variables (Figma forces pixel units). Set line-height directly as percentage on text styles.
figmaMapping.lineHeightConversion: Map semantic presets to percentages: display-tight≈110%, display-normal≈120%, display-loose≈140%, body-tight≈140%, body-normal≈170%, body-loose≈200%

colorSchemes.description: Color schemes are fully settings-driven with 6 default schemes plus custom ones. Each scheme has 35+ semantic color roles organized in groups.

schemeStructure.customSchemes: Added via theme editor with UUID keys (e.g., scheme-a90ef358-...)

semanticGroups.name: Essential
semanticGroups.notes: foreground often has alpha (e.g., #000000cf = 81% opacity). primary_hover can be completely different color from primary.

semanticGroups.name: Secondary button
semanticGroups.notes: Background is almost always rgba(0,0,0,0) — truly transparent. Hover backgrounds often have low alpha.

semanticGroups.name: Inputs
semanticGroups.notes: Backgrounds are often transparent or very low alpha on non-white schemes.

alphaColors.description: Many color roles use hex-with-alpha or rgba. This is a critical pattern — see figma-best-practices.md for how to handle in Figma variables.
commonPatterns: border: #0000000f (black at 6%) to #000000cf depending on scheme
hover backgrounds: very low alpha whites (#ffffff0a, #ffffff14, #ffffff5c)

adaptiveOpacity.description: Horizon calculates adaptive opacity values per scheme based on background brightness. Dark backgrounds get higher opacity values for overlays.
adaptiveOpacity.calculation: Based on whether background lightness is above or below a threshold

cssApplication.inheritance: Blocks can inherit parent section's color scheme via inherit_color_scheme: true setting, or override with their own.

figmaMapping.description: How to map Horizon color schemes to Figma variables
figmaMapping.collections: Three collections: Theme Colors (opaque base + alpha variants), Grey Scale (opaque + alpha variants), Color Schemas (aliases only, one mode per scheme)
figmaMapping.alphaVariants: MUST create alpha variant variables (e.g., Grey/900/81) for every semi-transparent color. Without these, Color Schemas aliases break — text becomes invisible.
figmaMapping.transparent: Include a Transparent variable (rgba(0,0,0,0)) for truly transparent values like secondary button backgrounds.
figmaMapping.modeApplication: Use setExplicitVariableModeForCollection on section frames to apply the correct scheme. Do NOT create separate variants for color scheme switching.

spacing.description: Spacing scale is hardcoded in CSS. Radii and border widths are settings-driven.

spacingScale.description: Defined in theme-styles-variables.liquid as CSS custom properties. NOT in settings — cannot be changed by merchants.

figmaMapping.spacingVariables: Create a Spacing & Layout collection from the CSS scale. Since values are hardcoded (not settings), use the actual px values: 0, 2, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 40, 48, 60, 80, 100.
figmaMapping.radiiVariables: Create radius/{name} variables from the settings values for the specific store.
figmaMapping.borderWidthNote: Border widths are small integers (0-4px). Store as part of the manifest foundations, apply directly to strokes.

sections.description: Horizon sections follow a consistent pattern with nested blocks, CSS-class-based styling, and settings-driven configuration.

colorSchemeApplication.multipleSchemes: Some sections have multiple color scheme settings (e.g., header has color_scheme_top, color_scheme_bottom, color_scheme_transparent)
colorSchemeApplication.backgroundLayer: Uses separate .section-background element for z-index stacking with shadows/overlays

blockNesting.description: Horizon uses deep block nesting. Sections contain blocks, blocks can contain other blocks.
blockNesting.pattern: section → static blocks → nested blocks (e.g., _product-card → _product-card-gallery + product-title + price)
blockNesting.colorInheritance: Nested blocks have an inherit_color_scheme boolean. When true, they use the parent's scheme.

layoutPatterns.gridSections: product-list, collection-list, main-collection use resource-list grid pattern
layoutPatterns.gridSettings: columns (2-6), mobile_columns, columns_gap, rows_gap — all configurable per section
layoutPatterns.heroLayout: Content overlays image via absolute positioning. Content direction (row/column), alignment (flex-start/center/flex-end), and gap are configurable.
layoutPatterns.mediaWithContent: Side-by-side layout with configurable content direction and gap. Stacks vertically on mobile.

sectionWidthOptions.description: Most sections have a section_width setting

icons.description: Icons are SVG files in assets/ rendered inline. Stroke width is controlled by a global CSS variable.

color.description: Icons inherit color from parent text color

figmaMapping.description: Create a single 24x24 Icon placeholder component with fill bound to Essential/Text. For a full icon system, each icon would need to be imported as a separate component — but for the design system foundation, a placeholder is sufficient.

additionalAtoms.name: Accelerated Checkout
additionalAtoms.reason: Horizon renders Apple Pay / Shop Pay buttons in product and cart pages

additionalAtoms.name: Variant Picker
additionalAtoms.reason: Combined swatch + dropdown selector used in PDP variant selection

prioritySections.reason: Primary above-fold section used on most homepages. Complex layout with overlay content on media.

horizontal_alignment.reason: Left/center/right placement of content overlay on hero image

vertical_alignment.reason: Top/center/bottom placement of content overlay on hero image

toggle_overlay.reason: Enables/disables media overlay layer — significant visual change but binary toggle

prioritySections.reason: Workhorse layout section for content + media pairings. Used heavily on homepages and landing pages.

media_position.reason: Flips which side the media appears on — fundamentally different composition

media_width.reason: Changes the proportion of media vs content columns

section_width.reason: Container width — repeats across all sections, no compositional change

extend_media.reason: Extends media to screen edge when section_width is page-width — significant layout impact

prioritySections.reason: Carousel hero section with multiple slides. Common homepage section.

content_position.reason: Content overlaid on slide vs placed below — entirely different layout

display_mode.reason: Full frame vs showing peek of adjacent slides changes the visual treatment

slideshow_controls_style.reason: Pagination indicator type — changes visual weight of bottom area

prioritySections.reason: Standalone product showcase section. Reusable across pages.

layout.reason: Media/content side placement — same pattern as media-with-content

prioritySections.reason: Grid/carousel of product cards. Core e-commerce section.

layout_type.reason: Three fundamentally different display modes for the same product collection

horizontal_alignment.reason: Content alignment within section header area

prioritySections.reason: Displays collection cards. Similar to product-list but with an extra bento layout.

layout_type.reason: Four layout modes — bento is unique to collection-list and creates asymmetric featured grid

prioritySections.reason: Generic media/content carousel. Reusable across pages.
Scrolling text/content ribbon. Common decorative section.
Blog post card grid/carousel. Shares layout_type pattern with product-list.

layout_type.reason: Same three layout modes as product-list

prioritySections.reason: Stacked/parallax slideshow variant. Visually distinct from standard slideshow.
Interactive image with product hotspot pins. Unique interaction pattern.

section_height.reason: Aspect ratios that fundamentally change image framing and composition

toggle_overlay.reason: Enables/disables overlay on the hotspot image

prioritySections.reason: Simple spacing/divider element. Quick to build, widely used.

alignment_horizontal.reason: Divider position when not full-width — changes visual balance

corner_radius.reason: Subtle visual option — square vs rounded ends

skipSections.reason: Freeform code injection — no meaningful component structure
Third-party app placeholder — no design to replicate

blockGrouping.description: Suggested grouping for universal blocks on the Figma Blocks page

quirks.description: Horizon-specific behaviors that generic heuristics might miss
quirks.adaptiveOpacity: Color schemes recalculate opacity variables based on background brightness. This is unusual — most themes use static opacity values.
quirks.separateBackgroundElement: Sections render background in a separate .section-background element (not on the section itself). This is for z-index control with shadows and overlays.
quirks.fontRolePerHeading: Each heading level independently selects which of the 4 font roles to use. H1 often uses 'accent' (display font) while H2-H6 use 'heading'. This is more granular than most themes.
quirks.semanticLineHeightPresets: Line heights are semantic strings (display-tight, body-normal) mapped to CSS values, not direct numbers. The analyze-theme skill must resolve these to actual percentages.
quirks.blockColorInheritance: Blocks have an explicit inherit_color_scheme toggle. This means a single section can mix color schemes across its blocks — unusual but important for complex layouts.
quirks.cssVariableFirst: Nearly zero hardcoded values in CSS. Everything flows from settings → CSS variables → applied via classes. This makes the theme highly configurable but means analyze-theme must read settings thoroughly.
